spec_match.rationale: The inspector still maps :playing, :paused, :seeking, :muted, and :volume-locked through CSS selectors on a real video element.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified play, pause, mute, unmute, and seek behavior on the local video fixture, including video:playing, video:paused, video:muted, and video.matches(':seeking') during the seeking event.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now matches its promise to provide a playable video control for inspecting media pseudo-class states.

edge_cases.rationale: The WebM fixture loaded cleanly with MP4 fallback still present, seek completion returned to a non-seeking state, and Chrome DevTools MCP found no console errors or failed network requests.

references.rationale: The page includes ChromeStatus, MDN, and Selectors Level 4 reference links.

design_system.rationale: The code sample was moved back to design-system variables and the page keeps the existing flat card layout.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verified video play, pause, seek, mute, unmute, media pseudo-class states, and clean console/network output for the local CC0 video fixture.
